summary: This post announces a book club for 'Occupy Finance,' a collaborative book produced by the Alternative Banking working group of Occupy Wall Street. The book club will meet weekly at Columbia University, with each chapter's author leading discussions on topics ranging from financialization to banking legislation failures. The group plans to incorporate feedback from these discussions into a second edition of the book. It's essentially an organizational announcement for activist education around financial reform.
key_points: Alternative Banking group created 'Occupy Finance' as a collaborative educational resource
Book club structure pairs chapter authors with readers for deep discussion
Topics cover the full spectrum of financial crisis causes: bailouts, securitization, legislative failures
Feedback loop designed to improve the book for a second edition
Meetings held at Columbia University, suggesting academic-activist partnership
money_quotes: We in Alternative Banking are pretty proud of our book Occupy Finance
Bailout: It didn't work, it's still going on, it's making things worse
A short history of the principal legislative failures that caused the crisis and make another one likely
Old Bankers' Tales … and Why to Reject Them
key_insight: Financial literacy and collective education are essential tools for grassroots movements challenging Wall Street's power.
